frontmatter.description: Deep investigation before planning. Use for best practices, implications, and systematic analysis.
(Compound) Perform comprehensive multi-pass code review with security, performance, and architecture checks.
Execute work plans systematically while maintaining quality. Use after /plan to implement features.
Handle PR comments and review feedback efficiently. Use when addressing reviewer feedback.
Orchestrate the full "plan → review → work → review → compound" lifecycle for small tasks.
(Compound) Transform feature descriptions into well-structured project plans with solution search and pattern checks.

frontmatter.name: insecure-defaults
frontmatter.description: "Detects fail-open insecure defaults (hardcoded secrets, weak auth, permissive security) that allow apps to run insecurely in production. Use when auditing security, reviewing config management, or analyzing environment variable handling."

frontmatter.name: retro
frontmatter.description: Run a session retrospective and route each finding to the right destination (GitHub issue / VIOLATIONS.md entry / episodic-memory MCP / direct fix / docs/SYSTEM.md). Use at session end, after a milestone, or whenever the session corrected/broke/learned something worth capturing. The value of this skill over un-aided reasoning is the destinations table — which prevents the most common failure mode of dumping cross-session facts (credentials, hostnames, ports) into VIOLATIONS.md instead of episodic memory.
frontmatter.allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Edit, Write, Bash(gh *)
